info.title: habis-finance-api Docs
info.description: Habis Finance API. Microfinance-grade Laravel API foundation with Sanctum authentication, strict request validation, durable idempotency, and standardized JSON responses.

get.summary:
Return the currently authenticated staff user with roles, permissions,
and agency context. Use this to refresh a session after role changes
without forcing a re-login

get.description: Returns a paginated client list scoped by agency permissions.

post.description: Creates a client profile inside the caller agency scope and reserves a client reference.

patch.description: Applies controlled KYC actions: submit, verify, reject, suspend, archive.
Supported actions: submit, verify, reject, archive, deactivate.

post.description: Links optional private KYC document evidence by public ID.
post.summary: Create a client identity document record

get.summary: Show a client identity document record

patch.summary: Update a client identity document record

patch.description: Supported actions: submit, verify, reject, archive.
patch.summary: Update client identity document lifecycle or verification status

get.description: Supports optional `current_only` filtering for currently active mandates.

patch.description: Supported actions: submit, verify, reject, archive, deactivate, expire.
patch.summary: Update client proxy lifecycle or verification status

path.description: Base path for paginator generated URLs.

per_page.description: Number of items shown per page.

to.description: Number of the last item in the slice.

total.description: Total number of items being paginated.

post.description: Uploads a KYC document file and creates a corresponding document record. The file is stored securely and metadata is tracked for integrity verification.

get.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific KYC document by its public ID. The file content itself is served by the separate `documents/{document}/file` endpoint.

Authorization mirrors `show`: the actor must hold `documents.view` and
be scoped to the document's agency, which blocks cross-agency access.
get.summary: Stream a document file for display/preview

patch.description: Archives a KYC document by changing its status to 'archived'. This changes the domain lifecycle state without physically deleting the underlying file.

get.summary:
Show a loan. Pass include_setup_charges=true to embed the reloadable
setup-charge readiness state under `setup_charges`, using the same
serializer as GET /loans/{loan}/setup-charges (FBI2-030)

parameters.name: include_setup_charges
parameters.description: Embed the setup-charge readiness state under data.setup_charges.

get.description:
Returns readiness status, assessed charges (with collectable /
blocking_disbursement / waiver_decision flags), insurance premiums and
their payments, and the required next actions before disbursement. Shares
the readiness rules enforced by loan disbursement.
get.summary: Reloadable loan setup-charge and insurance-premium state (FBI2-030)

get.summary:
FBI2-031 — report whether the agency/currency has usable approved mappings
for each loan posting operation, categorising blockers (missing, inactive,
unapproved, expired, cross_agency, overlapping) so the UI can warn before a
user reaches disbursement

parameters.name: agency_public_id
parameters.description: Agency to check. Non-platform users may only check their current agency; defaults to it.

get.description:
Returns the stable machine keys, display labels, required face counts,
and expiry requirements used to drive the frontend document-type select
and to validate identity-document submissions.

get.summary: Cash transaction tender options for teller deposit/withdrawal screens

get.description:
Returns every formula policy key with its category, label, approval
status, owner, approval date, and the loan-product fields it configures.
Unapproved policies are returned with `approved=false` so the UI can
disable them rather than guessing.
get.summary: Formula policy catalog for the loan-product UI

path.description: Base path for paginator generated URLs.

per_page.description: Number of items shown per page.

to.description: Number of the last item in the slice.

total.description: Total number of items being paginated.

put.description:
This intentionally replaces the entire set, so the frontend MUST send
the complete selected list. To make the destructive nature impossible to
miss, callers must send `replace=true`, the response reports
`previous_permissions`, `added_permissions`, and `removed_permissions`,
and an optional `expected_permissions_version` guards against a stale
editor baseline silently overwriting a newer save (AIR-001). For
single-checkbox toggles, prefer the grant/revoke endpoints.
put.summary: Full-replacement update of a role's permission set

post.summary:
Additively grant a single permission (checkbox toggle on). Never revokes
any other permission, so it cannot silently strip a role (AIR-001)

delete.summary:
Subtractively revoke a single permission (checkbox toggle off). Only the
named permission is removed; platform-admin minimums cannot be revoked

get.summary: List guarantors across the institution or current agency
List proxies/mandates across the institution or current agency

identity_redacted.description:
Frontend redaction flags (FB-PII-002 / FB-FE-001). These let the
frontend distinguish masked values from genuinely missing data.
- identity_redacted: operational identity/contact is masked.
- sensitive_pii_redacted: full sensitive PII is hidden.
- pii_redacted: transitional alias kept for backward compatibility;
tracks the full sensitive PII tier (crm.pii.view).

message.const: EMF ledger account mappings retrieved successfully
EMF ledger account mappings retrieved successfully

account_number.description:
Optional: when omitted, the backend reserves the next ACC########
value from the configured account sequence. A client-provided
value is still accepted and must be unique.

business_date.description:
Optional for backward compatibility; if supplied it must equal the
open accounting day's business date (enforced by AccountingDayGuard).

Optional: the open accounting day governs the business date. When
supplied it must equal the open day (enforced by AccountingDayGuard).

source_module.description:
business_date is intentionally not updatable: it is controlled by the
accounting day the draft was created under (JC-004).

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
